sources.title: Centessa Q3 2025 Financial Results and ORX750 Update

asset.name: ORX750
asset.one_liner: First oral OX2R agonist to demonstrate efficacy across narcolepsy type 1, type 2, and idiopathic hypersomnia

biology.simple_explanation: Orexin neurons in the hypothalamus produce orexin-A and orexin-B neuropeptides that bind to OX1R and OX2R receptors. OX2R activation promotes wakefulness, stabilizes sleep-wake transitions, and supports arousal. In narcolepsy type 1, orexin neurons are destroyed by autoimmunity, causing severe daytime sleepiness and cataplexy.
biology.pathway_detail: Orexin-A and orexin-B are produced by a small cluster of ~70,000 neurons in the lateral hypothalamus. These neurons project broadly throughout the brain, activating wake-promoting centers (locus coeruleus, tuberomammillary nucleus, raphe nuclei, ventral tegmental area). OX2R is the primary receptor mediating wake promotion. Loss of orexin signaling destabilizes the sleep-wake boundary, causing inappropriate transitions into sleep (sleep attacks) and REM intrusion (cataplexy, hypnagogic hallucinations).
downstream_effects: Potential neuroprotective effects via metabolic support

why_good_target.clinical_validation: The orexin pathway is genetically validated: narcolepsy type 1 is caused by loss of orexin neurons. Orexin receptor antagonists (suvorexant, lemborexant) are approved sleep aids, proving the pathway is druggable in both directions.

genetic_validation.loss_of_function: Loss of orexin neurons causes narcolepsy type 1 with cataplexy
genetic_validation.human_genetics: HLA-DQB1*06:02 associated with narcolepsy supports autoimmune destruction of orexin neurons

narcolepsy_type_1.cause: Autoimmune destruction of orexin-producing neurons (>90% loss)
narcolepsy_type_1.key_symptoms: Excessive daytime sleepiness (EDS), cataplexy, sleep paralysis, hypnagogic hallucinations
narcolepsy_type_1.current_treatments: Sodium oxybate (Xyrem/Xywav), stimulants (modafinil), pitolisant - all symptomatic, none restore orexin

narcolepsy_type_2.cause: Unclear etiology - may involve partial orexin deficiency or receptor dysfunction
narcolepsy_type_2.current_treatments: Limited - mostly off-label stimulants. No approved disease-specific therapies.
narcolepsy_type_2.unmet_need: HIGH - NT2 is underdiagnosed and undertreated

idiopathic_hypersomnia.key_symptoms: Severe daytime sleepiness, prolonged sleep duration, severe sleep inertia (sleep drunkenness)
idiopathic_hypersomnia.current_treatments: Xywav recently approved - first and only approved therapy
idiopathic_hypersomnia.unmet_need: VERY HIGH - historically no approved treatments

mechanism.how_it_works: ORX750 is a potent, selective, orally bioavailable agonist of the orexin receptor 2 (OX2R). By directly activating OX2R, it mimics the wake-promoting effects of endogenous orexin, restoring the signal that is lost or impaired in narcolepsy and hypersomnia.
mechanism.selectivity: Selective for OX2R over OX1R - this is important because OX1R is more involved in reward/anxiety circuits, while OX2R primarily drives wakefulness

lead.name: Narcolepsy Type 1 (NT1)
lead.rationale: NT1 has complete loss of orexin signaling - the most direct indication for an OX2R agonist

expansion.name: Narcolepsy Type 2 (NT2)
expansion.rationale: Massive unmet need - no approved disease-specific therapies. ORX750 could become new standard of care.

expansion.name: Idiopathic Hypersomnia (IH)
expansion.rationale: First OX2R agonist to show statistically significant efficacy in IH. Only one approved therapy (Xywav).

crystal_1_phase2a.enrollment: 55 participants across NT1, NT2, and IH
crystal_1_phase2a.key_efficacy_measures: Maintenance of Wakefulness Test (MWT), Epworth Sleepiness Scale (ESS)

nt1.headline: >20-minute increase in mean sleep latency on MWT vs placebo at 1.5 mg (p=0.0026)
nt1.detail: Half of NT1 patients achieved >30 minutes on MWT at 1.5 mg dose

nt2.headline: Clinically meaningful wake promotion observed
nt2.detail: Improvements could potentially establish ORX750 as new standard of care in NT2
nt2.significance: First OX2R agonist with meaningful NT2 data

ih.headline: First OX2R agonist to show statistically significant improvements in IH
ih.detail: Significant and clinically meaningful improvements on multiple efficacy measures including MWT
ih.significance: Breakthrough for a disease with minimal treatment options

crystal_1_phase2a.dose_response: Dose-dependent improvements observed across indications - 1.5 mg showed stronger signal than 1.0 mg

safety.overall: Generally well-tolerated. All TEAEs transient and mild-to-moderate.
safety.discontinuations: Not specifically reported as a concern

market_opportunity.narcolepsy_market: Global narcolepsy market estimated at $4-5B by 2030
market_opportunity.ih_market: Emerging market - Xywav is first approved therapy, significant room for better options
market_opportunity.unmet_need: NT2 and IH are severely underserved. An oral, well-tolerated OX2R agonist could transform treatment paradigm.

competitive_landscape.differentiation: Takeda has broader resources but ORX750 has shown efficacy across all three populations
Standard of care but requires nighttime dosing, has abuse potential, complex REMS program
Oral but modest efficacy compared to ORX750's direct orexin pathway restoration

catalysts.what_to_watch: Study design, endpoint selection, dose chosen for registrational study
Higher dose efficacy and safety to further characterize dose-response
